assertions.title: LM Studio is accessible at localhost:1234

steps.expected: HTTP 200 and non-empty data field with models list
steps.observed: HTTP 200 returned with 7 models in data array

assertions.title: Health check endpoint tests LM Studio connectivity

steps.action: Search for health endpoint in backend API
steps.expected: Find dedicated inference health check endpoint
Status healthy with LM Studio connection details
steps.observed: Returns healthy status with url and model info

assertions.title: qwen3.5-4b model is available and responds to streaming

steps.action: Check model is in list from /v1/models
steps.observed: qwen3.5-4b is present in the models list
HTTP 400 error - 'Failed to load model qwen3.5-4b'

error.message: Failed to load model "qwen3.5-4b". Error: Failed to load model.

streamingTest.note: Model is listed but not currently loaded in LM Studio memory

issues.blocker: Model qwen3.5-4b is listed but not loaded in LM Studio. Manual model loading required before streaming tests can pass.

steps.expected: Setting set to false or not present
steps.observed: Setting is NOT present in backend/.env file
Defaults to True (thinking disabled by default)

evidence.actualBehavior: Since .env doesn't set the value, config.py defaults to True, meaning thinking is DISABLED

issues.failure: backend/.env is missing LM_STUDIO_DISABLE_THINKING=false. Current effective configuration has thinking disabled (config.py defaults to True).
issues.recommendation: Add LM_STUDIO_DISABLE_THINKING=false to backend/.env to enable thinking functionality

frictions.description: Model qwen3.5-4b is listed in LM Studio but not loaded in memory, causing streaming test to fail with 'Failed to load model' error
frictions.resolution: Manually load the model in LM Studio UI before testing

frictions.description: Configuration inconsistency: .env.example recommends LM_STUDIO_DISABLE_THINKING=false, but backend/.env doesn't set it, and config.py defaults to True
frictions.impact: Thinking is effectively disabled despite being a required feature

blockers.description: Model not loaded in LM Studio - streaming test cannot complete
blockers.workaround: Load qwen3.5-4b model manually in LM Studio and re-run test

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-CFG-001, VAL-CFG-002), 1 blocked (VAL-CFG-003), 1 failed (VAL-CFG-004). LM Studio is accessible and health check works correctly. However, the qwen3.5-4b model is listed but not loaded in memory, blocking streaming verification. Most critically, thinking is effectively disabled due to missing LM_STUDIO_DISABLE_THINKING=false in backend/.env - the config.py defaults to True when the env var is absent.
